narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as riskier than we'd hoped—landing at a 53.8% chance of not passing. Picture your profile coming together like chapters in a story: the strongest thread was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, which stood out as the biggest signal pulling toward challenges ahead, suggesting those later grades didn't climb the way the model often sees in students who make it through. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 28.0, which echoed a similar concern, as lower scores like yours have historically tipped the scales toward failure in patterns we've observed. Your Year 1 decile of 1.0 layered on another early hurdle, reinforcing that starting point without much upward momentum. Even with your middle family income, which might offer some stability, it didn't counterbalance enough in the model's view, adding its own subtle push toward risk. As an Asian female studying full-time with a 2.2 undergrad GPA, these details all wove in as additional factors tilting the picture toward struggle, creating a combined portrait of someone facing steeper odds. It's not that any one thing defines you—it's this mosaic that made the model cautious about your bar passage likelihood. The good news? You're not locked in; focusing on boosting those grades or prepping intensely for the LSAT could rewrite your story for future applications. You've got resilience in targeting law school despite these numbers, and that's a strength worth building on. Hang in there—this is just one snapshot, and your next chapter can shine brighter. With encouragement, [Your Advisor]
